question: How many times is the compensation handler of a multi-instance activity invoked when a compensation throw event in its scope fires?

options.text: Once in total, regardless of how many items were in the input collection.
Once per completed instance of the multi-instance activity.
Once per item in the input collection, including any instances that failed.
Never — multi-instance activities cannot be compensated.

a.text: The compensation handler of a multi-instance activity is invoked only once, rather than for each item in the input collection. That single handler is responsible for reverting all instances.

b.text: To compensate each instance separately, the compensation handler itself must be marked as multi-instance. By default, the handler runs only once.

c.text: The handler is not invoked per item by default, and compensation is only triggered once all instances of the multi-instance activity have completed.

d.text: Multi-instance activities can be compensated; the handler is just invoked once unless it is itself multi-instance.

explanation: By default, a multi-instance activity's compensation handler runs once to revert all instances. The handler is only invoked when every instance has completed. Per-instance compensation requires marking the handler itself as multi-instance.
